P2 Theorem 1: <(e·z^)^{2n}> on srs 24 directed edges = 1/(3·2^{n-1}) for all n≥1. Proof: 8 edges perpendicular to any cubic axis (e·z^=0), 16 edges at 45° ((e·z^)²=1/2) under 432 chiral cubic symmetry. Rank-2 case Σ_e e_a e_b=(N_e/3)δ_ab is the foundation of Theorem 2.

P2 Theorem 2: CMB hemispherical power asymmetry A = ε/k = 1/15 = 6.67%. Two ingredients: eps_toggle = 1/5 from Bayesian Beta(1,1)->Beta(2,1) update (information-theoretic), and 1/k = 1/3 from Theorem 1 at n=1 (geometric). Both derived independently. Match to Planck 2018: 0.08σ.

P2 Theorem 3: B(P) has h = (√3+i√5)/2 as eigenvalue of multiplicity exactly 2 at the P-point of the primitive BZ. Multiplicity C_3-protected by the stabilizer of P in the 432 point group. Derivation: A(P) char poly = (λ²-3)² via sympy; Ihara-Bass translates to B(P) via factorization (2u²-√3u+1)². Singles out h uniquely among the high-symmetry points.

P2 Theorem 4: c_1(srs photon Hodge bundle) = 0 on every 2D slice of the BZ. Proof: T-symmetry d(-k)=d(k)* + self-conjugate slice argument + zero U(1) charge at the Γ defect (sphere integration verifies). Photon bundle topologically trivial in U(1) sense; bulk axion angle cannot source cosmic birefringence. Forces β to be dynamical.

Direct chirality reading: the walker's parity-odd phase content Im(h)/|h|=sin(arg h)=sqrt(5/8) leaks into photon polarization with coupling alpha_EM because c_1=0 on the photon Hodge bundle (P2 Thm 4) leaves the photon phase topologically unprotected. Mechanism is distinct from V_us/m_nu Feshbach perturbation: no self-energy integral, no coupling to ruliad Q-space — just direct chirality read-out enabled by the vanishing Chern class. Hard cap |β|≤α_EM≈0.418°; framework sits at 79% of this geometric bound. See dark_correction_theorem_2026-04-14.md §4c.1.
